title: Three Decades at the Aleksanteri Institute, Hegel, and Soviet Philosophy: Interview with Professor Emeritus Vesa Oittinen
content:
Emyleigh Simoes sits down with Professor Emeritus Vesa Oittinen to discuss his career at the Aleksanteri Institute, his forthcoming book project, and his contributions to Russian and Soviet intellectual history as a discipline.

Since joining the Institute shortly after its founding in 1996, Professor Oittinen has helped shape its research agenda while pursuing influential work on Russian modernization, German idealism, and Soviet philosophy. In this interview, he reflects on the institute's early years in the aftermath of the Soviet Union's collapse, discusses his forthcoming German-language book Hegels Dialektik der Moderne (Hegel's Dialectic of Modernity), and explains why Soviet philosophy—particularly the work of thinkers such as Lev Vygotsky and Evald Ilyenkov—deserves renewed scholarly attention. The interview provides a fascinating glimpse into Professor Oittinen’s pioneering role at Aleksanteri and the enduring relevance of studying Russian and Soviet intellectual history. 

The conversation below has been edited for length and clarity.

title: Helsinki University Museum Flame’s Art Room finds a temporary home in the Main Building
content:
The Porthania Building on the University of Helsinki’s City Centre Campus will be vacated in summer 2026 ahead of a renovation. For the duration of autumn 2026, the legendary Art Room will relocate to the Helsinki University Museum Flame’s exhibition centre in the University’s Main Building, where the temporary exhibition space has been reserved for its activities. 

The Art Room's stay in the Main Building offers a rare opportunity to explore the tradition of academic drawing instruction that stretches back to the University’s predecessor, the Royal Academy of Turku, in the 18th century. The exhibition will feature a selection from the Art Room’s plaster cast collection alongside intriguing examples of work by artists who have studied there, including pieces from what has been termed the Golden Age of Finnish Art and works by the current drawing master Vappu Rossi. The Art Room will be open to visitors from 16 September to 12 December 2026 during the opening hours of the Helsinki University Museum Flame’s exhibition centre: Tue–Wed 12.00–16.00, Thu 12.00–20.00, Fri–Sat 12.00–16.00.

title: Foodleader News: Highlights from May and June 2026
content:
In May, the practical course was successfully organized at UniLúrio from May 4th to May 13th. Dr. Noora Kanerva and Katri Salo travelled to Nampula to support the practical course implementation. In total, around 30 students participated in the course and studied the online materials on the DigiCampus platform between April 20th and May 3rd. The first days focused on reviewing the theory for the 24-hour recall and anthropometric measurements as well as practical practice on how to conduct these methods. On May 6th, the team visited the Nacua primary school, and the student got an opportunity to practice the methods with the schoolchildren. 

The remaining course focused on teaching the students how to analyse the data using the developed DASAT web app. The students analysed the data collected in September 2025 and prepared group presentations concentrating on different key nutrients or food hazards. On May 13th, we organized a stakeholder seminar at UniLúrio, where regional representatives from the primary schools were invited. The students successfully presented the results to the stakeholders, and a panel discussion was organized to further discuss the challenges and solutions.

title: Kesän puutarhaopastukset Kaisaniemessä
content:
On a guided tour, you’ll journey through millions of years of evolution along the paths of the Evolution Garden, and immerse yourself in a world of scents and colours in the Garden of the senses. You’ll also admire unique lichen and moss gardens, while hearing stories about the garden’s past and present, and the buildings found in the area.

Tours will take place from June 23 to August 22, 2026. Free entrance, no prior registration required.

  • Tuesdays, Fridays and Saturdays at 15 (3 pm)

    • The tour starts at the greenhouse entrance and lasts approximately 40 minutes.

    Kaisaniemenranta 2, Helsinki

title: Arkadiankadun siltaremontti aiheuttaa muutoksia liikennejärjestelyihin Luonnontieteellisen museon lähistöllä
content:
The renovation will start with the construction of temporary traffic arrangements from Wednesday 7 January onwards. The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

A temporary bridge for pedestrians will be built on the east side of Arkadiankatu over the Baana. The aim is to maintain the pedestrian route on the east side of the bridge until the temporary bridge is operational. Cyclists will also be able to use the bridge.

The Baana route underneath the bridge is expected to remain open for most of the renovation's duration, with some minor exceptions.

There will be changes to the motor vehicle routes by the bridge and around it in January. If possible, should be favoured during the bridge renovation.

Connections to the bus terminals in Kamppi will remain available, but there will be a slight diversion of routes.

Trams 1 and 1T will run via Simonkatu and Kamppi. Lasipalatsi and the Natural History Museum will not be served.

The diversion is due to the renovation of the Arkadiankatu bridge and will remain in place until late 2026. The renovation will not affect HSL's bus services. 

Pohjoinen Rautatiekatu 13

title: Ukrainan sota - yliopiston suositukset
content:
The University of Helsinki has condemned the military action by Russia and expressed its support for Ukraine and its university community. We have also expressed our support for Ukraine, together with other Finnish universities, through statements by and the . As a demonstration of support, we have illuminated buildings in the colours of the Ukrainian flag and raised the flag on our premises.

One of the principles of the University of Helsinki is to promote international collaboration in research and higher education, and to support these efforts even in difficult circumstances. Russia’s indefensible military aggression in Ukraine has required exceptional measures, which include those taken in the collaboration between universities.

The University of Helsinki is monitoring both national and EU-level sanctions, adapting our policies and processes accordingly. Together with other Finnish and international universities, the University of Helsinki strives to promote the end of military action in Ukraine, while supporting Ukrainian institutions and people.

title: Reflections on a workshop: Filippo Pedron at Stockholm University
content:
Filippo Pedron had the pleasure of being invited to give a talk at the workshop Philology Meets AI: Deciphering Rare Scripts, organized at Stockholm University in early May by the DESCRYPT Project. The workshop gathered experts in decryption and in the Elamite scripts and other rare scripts of the world, creating a great framework for discussion and exchange of ideas. 

Filippo drew on the experience gained from reading and translating Elamite texts for the WORK-IT project to give a talk on the adoption and adaptation of cuneiform writing for the Elamite language. He began by analyzing the first attested Elamite text, the treaty with the Mesopotamian king Narām-Sîn (2254–2218 BCE), comparing its script to those used in neighboring countries to determine how and when Elam adopted the cuneiform script from Mesopotamia. He then showed how the Elamites developed and adapted this script over time, up to its latest attestation during the Achaemenid Empire.
snippet: Filippo Pedron had the pleasure of being invited to give a talk at the workshop Philology Meets AI: Deciphering Rare Scripts, organized at Stockholm University in early May by the DESCRYPT Project.
creator: postjarn@helsinki.fi

title: Foodleader News: Highlights from March and April 2026
content:
The FoodLeader practical course, Food Safety and Nutrition Security for Sustainable Development, was successfully conducted at the University of Nairobi between May 16th to May 27th. Before participating in the course, students also studied online learning materials for learning the theory about food systems and sustainable development and dietary and food sample data collection as well as how to analyse and interpret the data via the DigiCampus Platform. Additionally, Dr. Noora Kanerva and Prof. Ndegwa Maina supported the practical course in Nairobi. 

During the course, students learned how to conduct anthropometric measurements and 24-hour recalls in practice. The students visited the local primary school, where they gained valuable experience interviewing and measuring the school-aged children to assess their nutritional status. The students also learned how to analyse mycotoxins and pesticides in food samples. They completed group work focusing on the different key nutrients or food hazards (For example: aflatoxin M1 (mycotoxin), imidacloprid (pesticide), metalaxyl (pesticide), total energy intake, zinc, and vitamin A).

title: Bioactivity Screening Group showcases anti-virulence research at ULLA Summer School 2026
content:
From 27 June to 4 July 2026, Maritta  participated in the European University Consortium for Pharmaceutical Sciences in Geneva, Switzerland. During the event, she presented her poster, "Broccoli water extract as a potential antivirulence inhibitor in uropathogenic Escherichia coli", showcasing the Bioactivity Screening Group's research on antivirulence strategies to fight microbial infections and antimicrobial resistance.

I spent a week full of science and networking in Geneva. I learned a lot, made new friends from universities around Europe, and was especially happy that the University of Helsinki won the traditional Geneva City Race. Receiving the winner's cup at the closing ceremony made the week even more memorable. Big thanks to the organising team!
